auditing-the-sales-kpi-dataset.label: Auditing the sales_kpi Dataset
auditing-the-sales-kpi-dataset.summary: Run focused checks for available categories, statuses, ranges, and missing values.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.title: Determine Average Customer Rating by Category
Check for Distinct Categories
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.prompt: Start the final capstone project. In step 1 of 5, Write a query to find all distinct categories in the `sales_reporting` table. This will help you understand the variety of products available.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.title: Identify Missing Customer Ratings
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 5, Write a query to find all records in the `sales_reporting` table where the `customer_rating` is missing. This will help you identify incomplete data.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.starterCode:
SELECT DISTINCT category FROM sales_reporting;
-- Capstone step 2: Identify Missing Customer Ratings
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to find all records in the `sales_reporting` table where the `customer_rating` is missing
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.solutionCode: SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE customer_rating IS NULL;

main_py.content:
SELECT DISTINCT category FROM sales_reporting;
-- Capstone step 2: Identify Missing Customer Ratings
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to find all records in the `sales_reporting` table where the `customer_rating` is missing
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE customer_rating IS NULL;

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.title: Calculate Total Sales per Region
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 5, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region in the `sales_reporting` table. This will help you understand regional sales performance.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.starterCode:
SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE customer_rating IS NULL;
-- Capstone step 3: Calculate Total Sales per Region
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region in the `sales_reporting` table
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.solutionCode: SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;

main_py.content:
SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE customer_rating IS NULL;
-- Capstone step 3: Calculate Total Sales per Region
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region in the `sales_reporting` table
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.title: Find Orders with High Discounts
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 5, Write a query to find all orders in the `sales_reporting` table where the discount percentage is greater than 10%. This will help you identify heavily discounted sales.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.title: Determine Average Customer Rating by Category
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 5 of 5, Write a query to determine the average customer rating for each product category in the `sales_reporting` table. This will help you assess customer satisfaction across categories.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.starterCode:
SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE discount_pct > 10;
-- Capstone step 5: Determine Average Customer Rating by Category
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to determine the average customer rating for each product category in the `sales_reporting` table
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_auditing_the_sales_kpi_dataset_sketc.solutionCode: SELECT category, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY category;

main_py.content:
SELECT * FROM sales_reporting WHERE discount_pct > 10;
-- Capstone step 5: Determine Average Customer Rating by Category
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to determine the average customer rating for each product category in the `sales_reporting` table
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
SELECT category, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY category;

sketch1.title: Introduction to the Capstone Project
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will take on the role of a data analyst tasked with auditing the `sales_kpi` dataset. Your goal is to prepare a comprehensive executive sales performance report. This involves checking for available categories, statuses, ranges, and identifying any missing values. By the end of this project, you will have a polished SQL query that provides insights into sales performance, ready for presentation to stakeholders.
